steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The text claims the spoiler enjoyment gap varied in degree across stories — some stories showed a larger gap than others. The correct answer pairs two stories whose gap sizes are clearly different from each other.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'An Occurrence at Owl Creek Bridge' vs. 'A Chess Problem': 'Owl Creek Bridge' has a notably larger spoiler gap than 'A Chess Problem,' making the variation in effect size obvious. ✓
  • Choice B — the paired stories: The two stories have similar gap sizes, so they do not illustrate variation in degree. ✗
  • Choice C — the paired stories: Same issue — gaps are too close to demonstrate the contrast. ✗
  • Choice D — the paired stories: Gap sizes again too similar to show meaningful variation. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
